title: Wrong comparison result with the != operator
body:
I wrote a serial multiplier and a test bench. The multiplier is correctly working and I compare the result against the product ( '*") operator as follows:

   always @(posedge done)
      begin
         tst = multiplicand * multiplier;
         if ( tst != result )
                tag = " error ***";
               else
                tag = "    ";
         $display(
                 "%d",$time,"         ",
                 "%d",multiplicand," ",
                 "%d",multiplier,"                 ",
                 "%d",result," ",
                 "%d",tst,"      ",
                 "%b",done," ",
                 "%s",tag," ",
                 "<-- result"
                );
        end

However when running a simulation even that the results are identical the tag is printed as "error ***" (see below). Even that the result 2 * -8 is equal to 16.

               40800           2 -8                  -16  -16      1  error *** <-- result

I worked around the problem by doing the comparison in a FORTRAN 2 style.
    always @(posedge done)
      begin
         tst = multiplicand * multiplier;
         if (!(tst-result))
           tag = " error ***";
         else
           tag = "    ";
         $display(
                 "%d",$time,"         ",
                 "%d",multiplicand," ",
                 "%d",multiplier,"                 ",
                 "%d",result," ",
                 "%d",tst,"      ",
                 "%b",done," ",
                 "%s",tag," ",
                 "<-- result"
                );
      end
And the problem does not show up.
               40800           2 -8                  -16  -16      1      <-- result

The problem also doesn't when using iverilator.

To reproduce the problem please compile using iverilog and verilog as follows:

make imult
make wmult
make mult

imult
wmult
mult

The third run (mult) will expose the problem.

The verilator version is

verilator --version
Verilator 4.016 2019-06-16 rev UNKNOWN_REV

This is what I get when install it by default in ubuntu as follows:

sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install verilator

Regards
S.R.

[serial_multiplier.tar.gz](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/4589552/serial_multiplier.tar.gz)
